gene_region.label: RTEL1 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical RTEL1 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: RTEL1 encodes a helicase that resolves telomere and genome replication structures. This topic bundle emphasizes aging and cellular maintenance, with interpretation centered on telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local RTEL1 bundle is oriented toward aging and cellular maintenance. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: RTEL1 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context
Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways
Progeroid, degenerative, cancer-risk, and stress-resilience review
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: RTEL1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for RTEL1.
Because RTEL1 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use RTEL1 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context
Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000258366: RTEL1 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: RTEL1 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: RTEL1: aging and cellular maintenance via telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a RTEL1 aging and cellular maintenance thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: RTEL1 aging and cellular maintenance pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: RTEL1 aging-maintenance variation model is curated as a RTEL1 marker for Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context, Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.

functional_effects: Can modify or tag telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.

variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair RTEL1 aging-maintenance variation model with RTEL1 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
